_instructions.how_to_use: Duplicate this file, rename it to your project slug (e.g. infrakit.json), delete all keys starting with underscore, and fill in every field. Import the completed file into your portfolio build.

field_guide.REQUIRED: Must be filled — card will break without it
field_guide.RECOMMENDED: Strongly encouraged — leave blank only if truly not applicable
field_guide.OPTIONAL: Nice to have, skip if it doesn't apply

meta._note: Administrative info. Not displayed publicly but used for ordering and filtering.
meta.slug: REQUIRED | URL-safe project ID, lowercase-hyphenated. e.g. 'cloud-clips'
meta.duration_weeks: RECOMMENDED | How long did it take? e.g. 5
meta.certs_applied: RECOMMENDED | Array of cert short names that are relevant. e.g. ['SAA', 'DVA', 'CloudOps']
meta.featured: OPTIONAL | true/false. Marks this as a hero/featured project.

header._note: Top section of the card. First thing a recruiter reads.
header.title: REQUIRED | Short, memorable name. e.g. 'CloudClips'
header.subtitle: REQUIRED | One sentence — what kind of system is it? Include the 2-3 most impressive technical adjectives. e.g. 'Serverless short-video platform with AI content moderation'
header.live_url: REQUIRED if live | Full URL. e.g. 'https://cloudclips.sokech.com'. Use null if not live.
header.github_url: RECOMMENDED | Full GitHub repo URL. Use null if private.

summary._note: The 'What it is' section. Write for a non-technical recruiter scanning in 8 seconds. No acronyms. 2-3 sentences max.
summary.plain_english: REQUIRED | What does it do? Who would use it in the real world?

motivation._note: The 'Why I built it' section. Write in your own voice, as if quoted. Shows intentionality.
motivation.quote: REQUIRED | 1-2 sentences starting with 'I' or a statement about the problem.

architecture._note: Describes the system design. The diagram is the most important artifact here.
architecture.diagram_path: RECOMMENDED | URL or relative path to architecture PNG/SVG. Use null to show placeholder.
architecture.diagram_alt: RECOMMENDED | Alt text for the diagram image.
architecture.flow_summary: REQUIRED | One-line data flow. Use → between steps.

highlights._note: The 'What I built' bullet list. 4-6 items. Each item must include WHAT you did AND WHY.
items: REQUIRED | Technology + the architectural decision behind using it

metrics._note: The 'By the numbers' grid. Max 4 metrics. Use real or estimated values — never fabricate.

stack._note: Tech stack tags, grouped by category. Each category renders in a different color.
stack.aws: REQUIRED if applicable | Array of AWS service names.
stack.concepts_patterns: OPTIONAL | Array of architectural patterns. e.g. ['Event-driven', 'Serverless']

challenges._note: The 'Challenges & learnings' section. THIS is what interviewers ask about. 3-5 items.
items: REQUIRED | What broke? What surprised you? What forced a design change?
REQUIRED | Last item should always be 'What I'd do differently: ...'

demo._note: Optional media attachments shown alongside the card.
demo.video_demo_url: OPTIONAL | Loom, YouTube, or direct .mp4 URL. Use null if none.
demo.readme_url: RECOMMENDED | Direct link to the GitHub README.
